memory_safety.description: unsafe block — review for memory safety violations (raw pointer deref, unchecked indexing, FFI)
Raw pointer dereference — verify pointer is valid and not dangling (only flagged in unsafe context)
slice::from_raw_parts — verify ptr is valid, aligned, and len is within allocation
slice::from_raw_parts_mut — verify no aliasing mutable references exist
mem::transmute — arbitrary bitcast; verify types have identical layout and validity
mem::transmute() — verify source and target types have same size and valid bit patterns
ptr::read/write — verify pointer is properly aligned and points to valid memory
ptr::copy — verify src and dst are valid, non-overlapping (for copy_nonoverlapping), and len is correct
Box::from_raw — takes ownership of raw pointer; double-free if called more than once on same pointer
Arc/Rc::from_raw — verify pointer came from Arc/Rc::into_raw to avoid refcount corruption

ffi.description: C FFI block — all functions inside are unsafe; verify argument types and lifetimes
CStr::from_ptr — verify pointer is non-null, points to valid null-terminated C string, and outlives CStr
CString::from_raw — retakes ownership; double-free if called on non-CString allocation
from_bytes_unchecked — skips UTF-8 validation; verify bytes are valid platform string

integer_safety.description: unwrap() panics on None/Err — use expect() with message or proper error handling in production
expect() panics — acceptable in tests/examples but verify not reachable in production code paths
Numeric cast with 'as' — truncates silently; use TryFrom/TryInto for checked conversion
Unchecked slice indexing — UB if index is out of bounds. Use get() instead

crypto.description: MD5 crate — insecure for security-sensitive hashing
SHA1 crate — insecure for security-sensitive hashing
thread_rng — not CSPRNG. Use OsRng or rand::rngs::OsRng for security tokens
Non-cryptographic RNG — do not use for security-sensitive values
AES-ECB mode — deterministic, leaks patterns. Use AES-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305

ssrf.description: reqwest::get with potential user-supplied URL — SSRF
HTTP client GET with variable URL — verify URL is not user-controlled
Hyper URI from variable string — verify not user-controlled

sql_injection.description: SQL query built with format! macro — SQL injection. Use parameterised queries
Database query with format! string — SQL injection. Use query_as! or bind parameters
sqlx::query with format! — SQL injection. Use sqlx::query! macro with bound parameters

path_traversal.description: Path::new from variable then join — verify no traversal sequences before canonicalize()
File operation — verify path is validated and restricted to expected directory
